questions.question: What grades does Pardes Day School serve?
questions.answer: Pardes serves students from 18 months in Preschool (Gan Katan) through 8th Grade. Our three divisions, Preschool, Elementary (K-5), and Middle School (6-8), each have their own leadership team while sharing one unified educational philosophy.

questions.question: What makes The Pardes Way different from other schools?
questions.answer: Every classroom has multiple teachers working simultaneously, with classes of 11 to 20 students and 3 to 4 teachers per room. Students receive direct instruction at exactly the right level every day through fluid instructional groups. Homework is reading review only, in both Hebrew and English. Everything else is done during the school day, so families get their evenings back. And through LeHashlim, our real-life skills curriculum, we teach collaboration, emotional management, and the skills the real world actually demands.

questions.question: What are the school hours?
questions.answer: Elementary (Mon-Thu): 8:15 AM - 3:55 PM. Elementary (Friday): 8:15 AM - 1:45 PM. Preschool / Gan Katan (Mon-Thu): 8:30 AM - 3:15 PM. Preschool / Gan Katan (Friday): 8:30 AM - 1:45 PM. Middle School / MSAP (Mon-Thu): 8:10 AM - 4:00 PM. Middle School / MSAP (Friday): 8:10 AM - 2:00 PM.

questions.question: How does the admissions process work?
questions.answer: Start by filling out an inquiry form. Our office will reach out to schedule a tour at a time that works for your family. After your tour, we schedule a parent conversation. We interview parents, not children, because the strongest partnerships start with understanding how families and school can grow together. Parents are welcome to bring their children to see the school. Admissions are rolling with no fixed deadlines. We accept families until we have space.

questions.question: Do you offer financial aid or scholarships?
questions.answer: Yes. Pardes accepts all major Florida scholarship programs including Step Up For Students, FES-UA, FES-EO, and FTC. We also offer need-based support through our Ahavas Yeladim Fund. We believe every family that wants The Pardes Way for their child should have a path to get here. Contact our admissions team at (305) 690-2548 to discuss options.

questions.question: What is fluid instructional grouping?
questions.answer: Instead of one teacher lecturing to the whole class, multiple teachers work with students in small groups that shift based on each child's current level in that subject. Groups are constantly reassessed using tools like NWEA MAP Growth and JSAT standards, so your child is always working where they'll grow the most.

questions.question: Do students have homework?
questions.answer: Homework at Pardes is reading review only, in both Hebrew and English. Practice, practice, practice. Everything else is done during the school day with the support of our teaching teams. If a student does not finish their work during school hours, they may take it home over the weekend, but that is the exception, not the rule. We believe family time matters.

questions.question: How does Pardes approach Jewish education?
questions.answer: Judaism at Pardes is something students experience, appreciate, and make their own. From Chumash and Kriah to Shabbat assembly and a love for Israel, our program weaves Jewish identity throughout the day.

questions.question: Is Hebrew language instruction included?
questions.answer: Yes. Hebrew is integrated throughout our curriculum starting in Preschool (Gan Katan). Our language program builds real proficiency and deepens students' cultural connection to Israel and Jewish traditions across every division.

questions.question: How does Pardes support students who learn differently?
questions.answer: Our fluid instructional grouping means every child already receives instruction at their level. Beyond that, we provide targeted learning support including tutoring and individualized educational plans when needed. Our team works closely with families to make sure no child falls through the cracks.

questions.question: What extracurricular activities are available?
questions.answer: Pardes offers sports, arts, music, Judaic clubs, robotics, and community service opportunities. Students also participate in holiday celebrations, theatrical productions, art exhibitions, and field trips.

questions.question: Where is Pardes Day School located?
questions.answer: Our main campus is at 1211 Marseille Dr, Miami Beach, FL 33141. Our middle school (MSAP) campus is at 7055 Bonita Dr, Miami Beach, FL 33141. You can reach us at (305) 690-2548 or office@pardesdayschool.org.
